Package neqsim.process.research

Class ProcessSynthesisGraph

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.research.ProcessSynthesisGraph
public class ProcessSynthesisGraph extends Object
P-graph-style material-operation graph for process synthesis.

Materials are graph nodes and OperationOption instances are operation hyperedges. This class enumerates bounded operation paths from a feed material to one or more product targets. It is deliberately lightweight: rigorous thermodynamics, convergence, and ranking remain in NeqSim process simulation, while the graph supplies systematic candidate topology generation.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team

  • Method Details

    • fromSpec

      public static ProcessSynthesisGraph fromSpec(ProcessResearchSpec spec)
      Creates a synthesis graph from a process research specification.
      Parameters:
      spec - process research specification
      Returns:
      populated synthesis graph

    • addMaterial

      public ProcessSynthesisGraph addMaterial(MaterialNode material)
      Adds a material node.
      Parameters:
      material - material node to add
      Returns:
      this graph

      Parameters:
      operation - operation option to add
      Returns:
      this graph

    • getMaterials

      public Map<String,MaterialNode> getMaterials()
      Gets material nodes.
      Returns:
      unmodifiable material map keyed by normalized material name

    • getOperations

      public List<OperationOption> getOperations()
      Gets operation options.
      Returns:
      unmodifiable operation list

    • enumeratePaths

      public List<List<OperationOption>> enumeratePaths(String feedMaterialName, List<String> targetMaterials, int maxDepth, int maxPaths)
      Enumerates operation paths from feed to any target material.
      Parameters:
      feedMaterialName - feed material name
      targetMaterials - target material names
      maxDepth - maximum number of operations per path
      maxPaths - maximum number of paths to return
      Returns:
      operation paths in discovery order

    • enumerateRecursive

      private void enumerateRecursive(Set<String> availableMaterials, Set<String> targetMaterials, List<OperationOption> currentPath, Set<OperationOption> usedOperations, Set<String> terminalMaterials, int maxDepth, int maxPaths, List<List<OperationOption>> paths)
      Recursively enumerates operation paths.
      Parameters:
      availableMaterials - currently available material names
      targetMaterials - normalized target material names
      currentPath - current operation path
      usedOperations - operations already used in this path
      terminalMaterials - materials produced by the previous path step
      maxDepth - maximum path depth
      maxPaths - maximum path count
      paths - accumulated paths

    • continuesSerialPath

      private boolean continuesSerialPath(OperationOption operation, List<OperationOption> currentPath, Set<String> terminalMaterials)
      Checks whether an operation continues the serial path represented by generated JSON.
      Parameters:
      operation - operation to inspect
      currentPath - current operation path
      terminalMaterials - materials produced by the previous step
      Returns:
      true if operation consumes the previous terminal material or starts the path

    • inputsAvailable

      private boolean inputsAvailable(OperationOption operation, Set<String> availableMaterials)
      Checks whether operation inputs are available.
      Parameters:
      operation - operation to inspect
      availableMaterials - available material set
      Returns:
      true if all declared inputs are available

    • containsAny

      private boolean containsAny(Set<String> left, Set<String> right)
      Checks for set intersection.
      Parameters:
      left - first set
      right - second set
      Returns:
      true if the sets share at least one item
